reason: The statement applies because the text fragment narrates the story of Naaman, a captain of the host of the king of Syria, who interacts with the prophet Elisha in Israel seeking healing from his leprosy. This interaction involves geopolitical elements such as diplomatic exchanges between the king of Syria and the king of Israel, as well as the acknowledgment of the power of the God of Israel by Naaman, a foreigner. The involvement of the LORD (Yahweh) in the healing process and the consequences of Gehazi's actions further highlight the geopolitical aspects of the narrative.
